input.description: Sync Prisma schema to local database

content.text: Dependencies are installed; I hit a PostgreSQL permission issue during Prisma sync, but I’ll still boot backend/frontend now so you can access the app and I’ll share exactly what DB fix is needed.

input.description: Check frontend dev server is reachable

content.text:
Project is set up and running locally now.

- Installed dependencies in `backend` and `frontend`.
- Created `backend/.env` with local defaults (`PORT=3001`, local Postgres URL, JWT secret, `FRONTEND_URL`).
- Started backend with `npm run dev` in `backend`.
- Started frontend with `npm run dev -- --host` in `frontend`.

Current local URLs:
- Frontend: `http://localhost:5200`
- Backend health: `http://localhost:3001/health` (returns `{"ok":true}`)

One issue still blocking full app data:
- PostgreSQL permissions are failing for `DATABASE_URL` (`User postgres was denied access on roamera.public`), so API routes that hit DB return 500.

Quick DB fix (Postgres):
```sql
CREATE DATABASE roamera;
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE roamera TO postgres;
```
Then run in `backend`:
```bash
npx prisma db push
```

After that, refresh `http://localhost:5200` and the data-backed pages should work.  
If you want, I can also set up seed data next (`node prisma/seed-all.js`) so the app has demo content.

<timestamp>Monday, May 4, 2026, 9:29 PM (UTC+5:30)</timestamp>
<user_query>
what are the features of this app? GIve me a detailed flow, explain the features and capabilities of the app. 
</user_query>
I’ll map this out feature-by-feature and then walk you through the end-to-end user flow based on the project docs so it’s clear how everything connects.

Absolutely — here’s a full product walkthrough of what this app does and how a user typically moves through it.

## What Roamera Is

`Roamera` is a **travel social + planning platform**.  
It combines:

- social journaling (posts, reactions, comments, follows),
- trip planning (destinations, flights/hotels, AI itinerary),
- group coordination (meetups, chats),
- money management (expense split + trip budget),
- and packing management.

It has both **web** and **mobile** clients backed by the same backend/API.

---

## End-to-End User Flow

## 1) Onboarding and Authentication

- User lands on welcome/onboarding screen.
- Can register with username/email/password.
- Email verification flow is supported (SMTP/Gmail-based setup in backend).
- Login returns JWT token; token is used for authenticated API calls.
- Session persists so user can come back without re-login (token validation via `/api/users/me`).

Capabilities here:
- secure auth (bcrypt password hashing + JWT),
- verified accounts,
- session continuity.

---

## 2) Home / Feed Discovery

After login, user enters a feed-driven experience:

- **Personalized feed** (based on network/activity),
- **Global/Compass feed** (broader discovery),
- quick access to create post, search, profile, planning tools.

Capabilities:
- continuous content exploration,
- social relevance from follows + activity.

---

## 3) Travel Journal Creation (Core Content)

Users create travel journals/posts with:

- destination/title/content,
- trip dates,
- budget values (INR-oriented),
- activity details,
- photos.

Journal pages include visual browsing (e.g., carousel-style media display).

Capabilities:
- rich storytelling,
- trip memory archiving,
- visual-first travel sharing.

---

## 4) Social Interaction Layer

For every journal/post, other users can:

- react with:
  - ❤️ Love
  - 📍 Wanna Go (adds to bucket list)
  - ✨ Inspire
- comment on posts,
- follow creators,
- receive notifications.

Capabilities:
- engagement loops,
- intent capture (`Wanna Go` becomes actionable wishlist data),
- social graph growth (followers/following behavior).

---

## 5) Bucket List and Destination Discovery

Two parallel discovery loops:

1. **Bucket list generation**  
   - Any `📍 Wanna Go` reaction can feed personal bucket-list curation.

2. **Trending destinations module**  
   - Browse popular places, often with category/filters.

Capabilities:
- passive discovery (trending),
- active curation (bucket list from reactions),
- helps transition from inspiration → planning.

---

## 6) Search & Exploration

Search supports finding:

- users,
- journals/posts,
- destinations-related content.

Capabilities:
- direct navigation to people/content,
- faster retrieval vs scrolling feed.

---

## 7) AI Trip Planner

User can invoke AI-assisted trip planning (Claude-powered in backend setup).

Typical flow:
- user inputs destination/preferences/duration,
- system generates itinerary suggestions,
- output can guide real trip planning and journal preparation.

Capabilities:
- automated itinerary drafting,
- personalized suggestions at planning stage.

---

## 8) TravelLens (Flights + Hotels)

`TravelLens` integrates external travel search (SerpAPI-based):

- search flights and hotels,
- compare options during trip planning.

Capabilities:
- planning from inside the same app,
- reduces switching between journaling and booking research tools.

---

## 9) Meetways (Group Trip Coordination)

For group travel, `Meetways` enables:

- meetup/trip plan creation,
- inviting participants,
- group interaction/chat.

Capabilities:
- coordination for multi-person trips,
- centralized planning communication.

---

## 10) JustSplit (Shared Expense Splitting)

Group cost management includes:

- creating travel groups,
- adding members,
- logging expenses,
- splitting amounts among participants,
- balance tracking and settlement actions.

Capabilities:
- transparent who-owes-whom accounting,
- trip finance handling without external split apps.

---

## 11) Journal Budget Tracker

Per-journal budget control:

- set/track spend by category,
- monitor totals vs remaining budget,
- visualize spending trend/progress.

Capabilities:
- financial discipline tied directly to each trip journal,
- useful for post-trip analysis and future planning.

---

## 12) Packing List Management

Per-journal packing module:

- create lists,
- categorize items,
- set quantity/essential markers,
- check off packed items,
- monitor packing progress.

Capabilities:
- readiness workflow for upcoming travel,
- checklist and organization for practical execution.

---

## 13) Mobile Experience

Mobile app is present with auth and app shell patterns documented; project docs indicate web-mobile alignment goal.

Capabilities:
- on-the-go account access,
- portable travel workflow (especially useful for journaling and trip-time usage).

---

## Feature Map by User Goal

- **“I want inspiration”** → feed, trending destinations, reactions.
- **“I want to document my trip”** → journals, photos, comments.
- **“I want to plan a trip”** → AI Planner + TravelLens + bucket list.
- **“I’m traveling with friends”** → Meetways + JustSplit.
- **“I need execution tools”** → Budget Tracker + Packing List.
- **“I want it anywhere”** → web + mobile clients.

---

If you want, I can next give you a **screen-by-screen flow** (exact route/page progression) for:
1) solo traveler journey, and  
2) group trip journey (from idea → booking research → expense split → post-trip journal).
